title: Oncall Handoff Continuity Checks
description: An incident-and-change response workflow that coordinates mitigation, approvals, and execution sequencing under operational urgency. Focus case: oncall handoff continuity checks.
assumptions: Operational artifacts (runbooks, mitigation plans, test environments) are pre-registered and discoverable.
Participants and approvers are identifiable and reachable via the system managing the workflow.
Actions are atomic state transitions that reflect authoritative updates to the session state.
Checklist semantics are deterministic: binding an item reserves it; releasing returns it to pool.
risks: Stale or incomplete runbooks could permit handoff finalization without adequate operational knowledge.
Race conditions when multiple actors claim or release the same checklist items or artifacts.
Missing external acknowledgements leading to unsafe change execution if not enforced.
Insufficiently granular stakeholder roles causing ambiguous approval responsibilities under urgency.
